report_name: NATO's Locked Shields 2026 Cyber Exercise Tests 4,000 Defenders Against 8,000 Live-Fire Attacks

campaigns.name: Locked Shields 2026
campaigns.description: A large-scale, live-fire cyber defense exercise organized by the NATO CCDCOE, involving over 4,000 participants from 41 nations defending a fictional country's critical infrastructure against approximately 8,000 real-time attacks. The 2026 scenario included defending an electronic voting system for the first time.
campaigns.objective: To practice and improve the defense of national IT systems and critical infrastructure, including electronic voting systems, in a realistic, high-pressure environment.

identities.name: NATO
identities.description: North Atlantic Treaty Organization, an intergovernmental military alliance whose CCDCOE organizes the Locked Shields exercise.

identities.name: NATO Cooperative Cyber Defence Centre of Excellence (CCDCOE)
identities.description: The organizer of the Locked Shields 2026 cyber defense exercise, based in Tallinn, Estonia.

identities.name: Berylia
identities.description: A fictional nation whose critical infrastructure (including power grids, 5G networks, and an electronic voting system) and IT systems were the target of simulated attacks during the Locked Shields 2026 exercise.

relationships.source_name: NATO Cooperative Cyber Defence Centre of Excellence (CCDCOE)
